## [15:08 - 18:09]

you indicate to others around you that you're changing as a person. And often this can freak those we are the closest to out. And this is why neutral ears is so important. ## [18:09 - 21:12]

Now, of course, we're going to want our family members and those that we love to support us on our journey as we aim to improve our communication skills. You've got to prime them. And what I mean by this is, again, you can't immediately just try new behaviors with those who have preconceived ideas of how you should sound. What you want to do is you prime them. You can say something simple as whether it's to your family or those that you work with. You can say, hey, look, I'm currently on a journey. I want to improve the way I communicate. I don't want to be so lazy with my voice. I want to be more engaging. And I'm telling you this because I was hoping you'd support me on this journey. Do you mind just calling me out if I'm being lazy with my voice? You know, I'm naturally quiet. Can you just call me out if I'm not using enough volume? And by saying something simple as that, it changes the way they perceive your new behaviors. Whereas before, they didn't have context you were trying to improve. All of a sudden, you come home and you're being ridiculous. And to them, they'll be like, oh, I don't like that. That makes me feel very uncomfortable because, again, it's indicating change. And most people, let's face it, don't like change. Whereas the moment you prime them, that allows them to perceive the new behaviors that you're trying out from a different perspective.
